title: Facilities, Racks, Cabling, Power, and Environment · Guided Evidence Lab
summary: Plan an MDF, IDFs, racks, patching, power, cooling, security, and maintenance access for a new site. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a facilities-ready network installation package while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Place MDF and IDF spaces, rack units, pathways, grounding, clearances, and service loops from building constraints.
Document patch panels, fiber distribution panels, labels, polarity, ports, pathways, and change ownership.
Align port-side intake or exhaust, hot and cold airflow, lockable access, and maintenance reach.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed workshop case derived from this module: network physical installation. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed workshop case derived from this module: network physical installation. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. What must be established before implementation?

content.text: Place MDF and IDF spaces, rack units, pathways, grounding, clearances, and service loops from building constraints. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network physical installation. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Mdf Idf Racks, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a facilities-ready network installation package while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: An IDF is simply a smaller rack and needs no distance or power planning. This fails the Net Mdf Idf Racks contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: CompTIA Network Plus N10-009 Exam Objectives (N10-009 v4.0) — Controls every certification domain, objective, acronym, tool, protocol, and performance-based scope decision.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Place MDF and IDF spaces, rack units, pathways, grounding, clearances, and service loops from building constraints.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-environment-fire-1n0it3" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Environment Fire into observable performance evidence.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Net Environment Fire condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Environment Fire applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Monitor temperature and humidity and select compatible detection, suppression, water, dust, and contamination controls.
